name: Decide CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED: flip on (tables landed via 0113) or confirm deferral pending cutover profiles→person FK repoint
description: Surfaced by the MMT-ADR-0017 re-vet (WI-752). The concept-grain mastery additive layer (concepts + concept_mastery) is fully built — schema (landed via migration 0113), writer captureConceptMastery() (apps/api/src/services/concept-capture.ts), reader wired end-to-end (notes/concept-mastery API → mobile useConceptMasterySignals → topic/book screens) — BUT the writer is permanently gated off: CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED = false (apps/api/src/services/concept-capture.ts:19). So zero concept_mastery rows are written in any environment and the reader always returns empty; the feature is dormant. History: the flag was set false when migration 0107 was reference-only (tables didn't exist → 'relation concepts does not exist' Sentry noise); migration 0113 has since reconciled the tables, but the flag was never flipped back. DECIDE: (a) flip CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED on to activate the dormant feature, or (b) formally confirm continued deferral with the gating reason + the trigger that would later enable it. CRITICAL dependency: concept-mastery FKs to `profiles` (not `person`); the design spec warns that enabling capture before the identity cutover's profiles→person FK repoint guarantees rework inside the cut — so this must be sequenced against/after that repoint (WS-18 Identity Cutover). If flipping on, also update the architecture.md canon line (currently 'gated behind CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED, currently disabled, pending the identity-cutover profiles→person FK repoint') lockstep to reflect the new state.
acceptanceCriteria: A recorded decision: EITHER CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED is flipped to true — with the cutover profiles→person FK repoint satisfied, concept_mastery rows verified writing on Challenge-Round completion, and the architecture.md canon line updated lockstep — OR continued deferral is formally confirmed with the gating reason and the trigger that would enable it. The profiles→person FK dependency is explicitly accounted for in the sequencing.
